upcoming_deadlines.title: BIS Affiliates Rule — Snap-Back Takes Effect
upcoming_deadlines.description: The temporary exemption from the BIS Affiliates Rule expires. Full restrictions on sharing Export Administration Regulations-controlled technology and software with foreign affiliates—including those in allied nations—become enforceable. Affected companies must have foreign-affiliate compliance programs, authorization records, and technology-control plans in place before this date.
upcoming_deadlines.authority: U.S. Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS)
upcoming_deadlines.regulation: Export Administration Regulations (EAR) — Affiliates Rule (15 CFR Part 734/736)
upcoming_deadlines.action_required: Audit foreign-affiliate technology-sharing arrangements; implement or update Technology Control Plans; confirm EAR authorizations are on file for all controlled items shared with foreign affiliates.

compliance_note: Export controls are enforced across multiple jurisdictions. Monitor BIS (US), UAE IEC (Emirates), DOJ (enforcement), and international regimes (Wassenaar) for changing restrictions on technology, equipment, and services.
